verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B (ATP synthase F1 beta subunit) is canonically a mitochondrial inner-membrane matrix-facing protein, part of the F1 catalytic head of ATP synthase. However, ectopic surface expression of ATP synthase components (including the beta subunit) on the plasma membrane of hepatocytes, endothelial cells, adipocytes, and notably tumor cells has been documented by surface biotinylation, flow cytometry on intact cells, and antibody binding studies. Ecto-F1-ATPase serves as a receptor for apoA-I, angiostatin, and coupling factor 6, and anti-beta-subunit antibodies (e.g., HUVEC-binding) have been developed as antiangiogenic/antitumor agents. This places it in cell_state_induced contextual surfacing.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Mechanism of mitochondrial-to-PM trafficking of ecto-F1-ATPase remains unclear; uncertain if surface pool is robust enough for therapeutic targeting vs. tissue_restricted_surface.
